2603.19954.title: On the Ability of Transformers to Verify Plans
2603.19954.paper_summary: Transformers have shown inconsistent success in AI planning tasks, and theoretical understanding of when generalization should be expected has been limited. We take important steps towards addressing this gap by analyzing the ability of decoder-only models to verify whether a given plan correctly solves a given planning instance. To analyse the general setting where the number of objects -- and thus the effective input alphabet -- grows at test time, we introduce C*-RASP, an extension of C-RASP designed to establish length generalization guarantees for transformers under the simultaneous growth in sequence length and vocabulary size. Our results identify a large class of classical planning domains for which transformers can provably learn to verify long plans, and structural properties that significantly affects the learnability of length generalizable solutions. Empirical experiments corroborate our theory.
2603.19954.paper_summary_zh: Transformer在人工智慧規劃任務中的成功表現不一致，對於何時應該期待泛化的理論理解也有限。<lb/>我們通過分析僅解碼器模型的能力，採取重要步驟來解決這一差距，以驗證給定計劃是否正確解決了特定的規劃實例。<lb/>為了分析在測試時物體數量（因此有效輸入字母表）增長的一般情況，我們引入了 C*-RASP，這是 C-RASP 的擴展，旨在在序列長度和詞彙大小同時增長的情況下建立Transformer的長度泛化保證。<lb/>我們的結果確定了一大類經典規劃領域，對於這些領域，Transformer可以明確學會驗證長計劃，以及顯著影響可學習長度泛化解決方案的結構特性。<lb/>實證實驗證實了我們的理論。
2603.19954.author: Yash Sarrof et.al.
2603.19954.authors: Yash Sarrof, Yupei Du, Katharina Stein, Alexander Koller, Sylvie Thiébaux, Michael Hahn
